content:
If that error is 'Unable to log in at server. Probably wrong configuration, username or password',

- Make sure cookies are allowed in Thunderbird

- Do not use a VPN while attempting to authenticate your Google account

- Make sure there is no application running on your computer which is blocking local TCP port 80, e.g. a local web server

Se anche sul server via webmail trovi lo stesso numero di messaggi, allora la sincronizzazione tramite IMAP funziona correttamente. IMAP infatti server a tenere sempre sincronizzato il server con tutti i client.

Il problema a questo punto è capire dove vengono cancellati i messaggi e per quale motivo. Prova ad esempio a non utilizzare Thunderbird per qualche giorno e vedi se sulla webmail i messaggi restano tutti visibili.

content:
Very unlikely in my opinion. They are installed in separate locations and share no files.

Without knowing anything pertinent about your crashing problem, the first and easiest thing to try is to disable Thunderbird's Hardware Acceleration feature. It can be found at <Settings | General>, scroll all the way to the bottom, uncheck the "Use hardware acceleration when available" box. The option is designed to improve performance but, in my experience, doesn't make much difference and can clash with some display drivers.

content:
Unfortunately, this is not a built-in feature of Thunderbird. I wish it were - it could be very useful to me, too. The closest thing it has is the Tags feature, but that doesn't allow for free form notes.

However, there is at least one add-on that claims to do just that, QNote. There may be others.

https://addons.thunderbird.net/en-US/thunderbird/addon/qnote/

content:
You can create a separate profile for each of you. This way, when you start Thunderbird you will first see the Profile Manager and can choose which of two to use for that session.

Here's an article about Profiles and how to use them:

https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/profile-manager-create-and-remove-thunderbird-profiles
